seo.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard Reference & Video-to-Video Generator | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard for lower-cost reference-guided Kling video, storyboard images, source-video V2V, @Image and @Video1 anchors, optional audio, and clear start-frame separation.

overview: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard is the lower-cost Kling Omni route for reference-to-video, storyboard drafts, and source-video video-to-video experiments.
pricingNotes: Provider cost is about $0.126/s with audio on and $0.084/s audio off before the MaxVideoAI margin.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Use Standard for lower-cost O3 reference drafts, Pro when source-video V2V and stronger continuity matter, and Kling 3 Standard when the image must become the first frame.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro
relatedItems.description: Move to Pro when the draft needs stronger storyboard continuity, source-video V2V, native audio, and more production-ready reference control.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 Standard
relatedItems.description: Use Kling 3 Standard when the uploaded image should visibly open the clip instead of acting as a detached reference or storyboard guide.

relatedItems.title: Seedance 2.0
relatedItems.description: Compare against Seedance when the decision is multi-shot continuity, native audio, and storyboard-to-video behavior across scenes.

custom.finalPara1: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard is the practical first stop for reference-guided experiments: test storyboard images, style anchors, and source-video motion without paying for final 4K delivery on every iteration.
custom.finalPara2: When a Standard draft validates the composition, move the same reference plan to Kling 3.0 Omni Pro for stronger continuity or to 4K for final delivery.

hero.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard for lower-cost reference-guided video
hero.intro: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard when images should guide style, subject, or storyboard direction without becoming the first frame, or when one source video should guide motion via V2V. Use Kling 3 Standard when the uploaded image should visibly open the clip.
hero.badge: Reference images · Source video V2V · 1080p · Audio controls

items.title: Reference-guided drafts before a Pro pass
Storyboard images that should not become the first frame
Cost-controlled source-video V2V and style reference tests

technicalOverview.label: Reference images
technicalOverview.body: Upload 1-4 reference images and mention them as @Image1, @Image2, etc. Use the prompt to define whether each image controls style, subject, shot composition, or storyboard continuity.

technicalOverview.label: Source-video V2V
technicalOverview.body: Upload one 3-10 s source video for video-to-video and mention it as @Video1. Add @Image references for style, and use keep_audio when the source soundtrack should be preserved.

technicalOverview.label: Start frame separation
technicalOverview.body: Reference mode does not require the first image to become the opening frame. Use image-to-video mode only when you want a true start frame.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Kling 3.0 Omni Standard references
promptStructure.quote: Use @Video1 for pacing, @Image1 for storyboard composition, and @Image2 for character style. Generate a short 1080p variation with consistent motion and optional ambience.
promptStructure.description: Write the prompt around the role of each visual input. Keep 3.0 Omni Standard for lower-cost reference and video-to-video tests, then promote the approved direction to 3.0 Omni Pro or 4K if needed.
steps: Choose Reference mode instead of Image-to-video
Upload one source video for V2V when motion, camera rhythm, or scene continuity should come from @Video1
Name each input in the prompt as @Image1, @Image2, etc.
Set keep_audio when the source-video audio should carry through the video-to-video pass
State whether each image controls style, subject, layout, or continuity
Use Kling 3 Standard only when the upload should become the start frame

faqs.q: Is this the same as Kling 3 image-to-video?
faqs.a: No. Kling 3 image-to-video is start-frame animation. Kling 3.0 Omni Standard reference-to-video is for references and storyboard guidance.

faqs.q: Does Kling 3.0 Omni Standard support video-to-video?
faqs.a: Yes. Standard supports source-video V2V with one 3-10 s clip referenced as @Video1, optional @Image references, and keep_audio when the original source audio should be preserved.

hero.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard
hero.subtitle: Lower-cost reference-to-video, storyboard drafts and source-video V2V before moving approved directions into O3 Pro.
hero.paragraph: Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard for cheaper reference-guided iteration: storyboard images, style anchors, optional start/end frames and source-video V2V tests. Move winners into O3 Pro when the shot needs stronger final polish.

features.title: Reference images
features.body: Use @Image anchors for style, subject, storyboard or scene continuity without forcing the first image to open.

features.title: Source-video V2V
features.body: Attach a source video and refer to @Video1 when motion, pacing or camera language should guide the shot.

features.title: Optional start/end frames
features.body: Combine references with opening or ending frames when the shot needs stronger structure.

features.title: Native audio route
features.body: Generate with audio when the selected O3 route exposes audio controls.

features.title: Up to 15s
features.body: Use Pro for longer reference-guided clips before moving final 4K work to the 4K route.

features.title: Live quote
features.body: The app shows the exact price before generation.

decisionCards.title: Standard or Pro?
decisionCards.body: Use Standard for lower-cost reference and V2V tests. Move to Pro when the selected direction needs final-quality fidelity.

decisionCards.title: Need source-video guidance?
decisionCards.body: Use O3 Pro or Standard for source-video V2V. Prompt with @Video1 when the source clip should control motion or camera continuity.

decisionCards.title: Need native 4K?
decisionCards.body: Use O3 4K after the reference direction is approved. Stay on Pro while testing source-video V2V and storyboard structure.

referenceWorkflows.title: Prompt only
referenceWorkflows.body: Use text when no visual anchor is needed and O3 should generate from the scene brief.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reference images
referenceWorkflows.body: Use @Image1, @Image2 and more for character, object, style or storyboard guidance.

referenceWorkflows.title: Start/end frames
referenceWorkflows.body: Add optional opening or ending frames when the first or last pose must be controlled.

referenceWorkflows.title: Source video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use @Video1 for video-to-video motion, pacing, composition or camera continuity.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reference plus V2V
referenceWorkflows.body: Combine source video and reference images when motion and visual identity both matter.

section.title: Prompt Lab — Kling 3.0 Omni Standard reference drafts
section.referencesTitle: How O3 Standard structures reference drafts

tabs.label: Text
tabs.title: O3 Standard text-to-video prompt
tabs.description: Use this to validate an idea cheaply without assets.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Character / product / place + 2 visual traits]

Action:
[One readable action or 2-3 short beats]

Camera:
[Camera move + framing + continuity]

Style:
[Lighting, texture, palette, cinematic look]

Audio:
[Native ambience, short SFX, brief dialogue if useful]

Example:
A designer crosses a night studio holding a glowing prototype. Camera: low tracking shot into a slight push-in. Style: blue reflections, brushed metal, premium finish. Audio: studio ambience, subtle electric hum, no subtitles.

tabs.label: Refs
tabs.title: O3 Standard reference-to-video draft
tabs.description: Use this to test style, subject, or storyboard without a forced start frame.
tabs.copy:
Optional opening frame:
[What should structure the first beat without overriding references]

Optional end frame:
[Expected final pose / composition / camera distance]

References:
@Image1 = [composition or character]
@Image2 = [style, lighting, or object]

Action and camera:
[One readable motion path between opening and ending]

Example:
Use @Image1 as the opening composition and @Image2 as the lighting style. The character walks slowly toward camera, then lands in a stable pose close to the end frame. Consistent light, no location change.

tabs.label: V2V draft
tabs.title: Cost-controlled O3 Standard V2V
tabs.description: Use this to test @Video1 and keep_audio before moving to Pro.
tabs.copy:
@Video1 = [camera rhythm / motion / pacing]
@Image1 = [storyboard or composition]
@Image2 = [character, product, or style]

Intent:
[What the video should generate without copying every source frame]

Continuity:
[What must not drift: face, product, location, lighting]

Audio:
[generate_audio or keep_audio depending on the goal]

Example:
Use @Video1 for camera rhythm, @Image1 for storyboard composition, and @Image2 for character style. Generate a 12s cinematic three-beat sequence with stable continuity, native ambience, and no added text.

tabs.label: Upgrade
tabs.title: Promotion plan for Pro or 4K
tabs.description: Use this to record what must stay locked after the draft.
tabs.copy:
Duration: [8-15s]

Shot 1 (0-4s):
[Composition guided by @Image1 + readable action]

Shot 2 (4-8s):
[Motion or camera inspired by @Video1]

Shot 3 (8-12s):
[Payoff / final pose / product insert]

Anchors:
[Style @Image2, wardrobe, location, lighting, audio]

Example:
Shot 1: @Image1 defines the scene. Shot 2: @Video1 guides camera speed. Shot 3: @Image2 locks the final style. Short native ambience, character and location continuity.

globalPrinciples: State whether each asset is a reference or a visible frame.
Use @Image1, @Image2 for images and @Video1 only for source-video V2V.
When @Video1 is loaded, the workflow becomes V2V; start/end frames should not drive the shot.
Keep one readable action per shot and one job per reference.
Use keep_audio only when the source soundtrack should remain.
engineWhy: Standard is the draft route for lower-cost reference and V2V tests.
Keep prompts shorter and compare one variable at a time.
Move to Pro for final fidelity or 4K after the direction is approved.
Even in draft mode, each @Image and @Video1 needs one clear job.

demo.title: Demo prompt — O3 Standard reference draft
demo.prompt:
Subject:
A giant origami whale made of folded newspaper, gliding through a dry European street like it is swimming underwater.

Action:
The paper whale passes between buildings, creating a wave of loose paper. People step back in surprise as the whale’s tail gently sweeps newspapers into the air.

Camera:
Low wide tracking shot moving alongside the whale, keeping the full body readable. Smooth forward motion, no sudden cuts.

Style:
Cinematic magical realism, soft afternoon light, paper textures, muted beige and blue-gray palette, elegant surreal mood, realistic city with a poetic fantasy element.

Audio:
Soft paper rustling, distant city ambience, deep whale-like paper creak, gentle wind swell.

summary.subject: Giant origami whale made of folded newspaper
summary.action: Glides through a dry European street and lifts loose paper
summary.camera: Low wide tracking shot alongside the whale
summary.style: Cinematic magical realism, soft light, paper textures
summary.output: Paper rustle, distant city ambience, gentle wind

section.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Standard reference drafts
